fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Tennessee Physical Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a physical therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Tennessee - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: physical therapist license reciprocity tennessee, how to transfer physical therapist license to tennessee, physical therapist compact tennessee, tennessee physical therapist endorsement application

board.name: Tennessee Board of Physical Therapy
board.mailing_address:
Tennessee Department of Health
Health Related Boards
665 Mainstream Drive
Nashville, TN 37243

license_types.title: PT, DPT
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a physical therapist in Tennessee.

steps.title: Create Account
steps.description: Visit the Licensure and Regulatory System (apps.tn.gov/health/) and create a new user account by clicking 'Create an Account' and following the registration prompts.

steps.title: Submit Online Application
steps.description: Log in to the Licensure and Regulatory System, select the appropriate Physical Therapist application type (initial or endorsement), and complete all required sections of the application form.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Remit the non-refundable application fee of $175 through the Licensure and Regulatory System using an accepted payment method.

steps.title: Complete Jurisprudence Exam & CBC
steps.description: Successfully complete the Tennessee Jurisprudence Examination online and fulfill the requirements for a Criminal Background Check (CBC) as instructed during the application process.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documents
steps.description: Arrange for all necessary supporting documentation, such as official transcripts, NPTE scores (if applicable), and verification of licensure from other states (for endorsement), to be sent directly to the Board.

steps.title: Board Review and Approval
steps.description: The Tennessee Board of Occupational Therapy and Physical Therapy Examiners will review your complete application and all submitted documentation. Upon approval, your license will be issued.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NPTE. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Tennessee physical therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Tennessee have reciprocity for physical therapists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Tennessee participates in the PT Compact. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Tennessee without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to Tennessee?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Tennessee while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Physical Therapist in Tennessee
hero.dek: To apply for a Physical Therapist license in Tennessee, visit the Tennessee Department of Health's Licensure and Regulatory System to create an account and submit your application online.

official_links.registration_steps: Visit the Tennessee Department of Health's Licensure and Regulatory System (apps.tn.gov/health/) and click 'Create an Account' for new users. Follow the prompts to set up your profile, including contact information and security questions, then verify your email to activate your account.
